description: Learnzy Growth Mindset — daily affirmations, challenges, and a fixed vs growth mindset tracker for children

for_child.intro: Your brain is like a muscle — the more you use it, the stronger it gets! Every time you make a mistake and try again, your brain grows new connections.

daily_challenges.title: Try Something Hard
daily_challenges.challenge: Do something today that you find difficult. Try for 5 minutes without giving up. Tell your buddy what you tried and how it went — even if you didn't succeed yet!
daily_challenges.buddy_prompt: Ask: What did you try? Was it hard? Did you want to quit? What kept you going? Celebrate the ATTEMPT, not just success.

daily_challenges.title: Mistake Moment
daily_challenges.challenge: Think of a mistake you made recently. Write or tell 3 things: what happened, what you learned, what you'll try differently next time.
daily_challenges.buddy_prompt: Normalise the mistake. 'Every scientist ever made mistakes — that's HOW science works! What did this mistake teach you that getting it right never would have?'

daily_challenges.title: Yet Power
daily_challenges.challenge: Write 3 things you cannot do YET. Add the word YET to each. Say them out loud: 'I cannot do this... YET.'
daily_challenges.buddy_prompt: Ask the child to share their 'yet' sentences. Respond: 'I believe you WILL be able to do all three of those! Which one feels closest to happening? What is one small step toward it?'

daily_challenges.title: Effort Spotting
daily_challenges.challenge: Today, notice when YOU put in effort — even on small things. Count how many times you tried hard. Then notice someone else trying hard and tell them!
daily_challenges.buddy_prompt: Ask: How many times did you try hard today? What was the hardest thing? Did you tell someone they were doing well? How did they react?

daily_challenges.title: Week Reflection
daily_challenges.challenge: Think back on this whole week. What is ONE thing you got better at? It can be anything — not just school work. Maybe you got better at being patient, or kind, or trying!
daily_challenges.buddy_prompt: Celebrate the growth enthusiastically. 'You improved at [X] this week — that is REAL progress! What made the difference? Can you keep doing that next week?'

daily_challenges.title: Teach Someone
daily_challenges.challenge: Pick something you know well and teach it to someone at home. Teaching is the BEST way to deepen your own understanding!
daily_challenges.buddy_prompt: Ask what they taught and to whom. 'What questions did they ask that you couldn't answer? Those gaps are your next learning targets! Teaching others makes YOU smarter.'

fixed_vs_growth.growth: I'm not good at maths yet — but I can improve with practice
fixed_vs_growth.situation: Getting a low mark on a test

fixed_vs_growth.growth: This is hard right now — it means my brain is working hard
I need a different strategy — let me try another way

fixed_vs_growth.fixed: I made a mistake, I'm so stupid
fixed_vs_growth.growth: I made a mistake — that tells me exactly what I need to practise more
She's worked harder at this than I have so far — I can do the same

affirmations: My brain grows stronger every time I try something hard.
Mistakes are not failures — they are information.
I am not comparing myself to others — only to who I was yesterday.
The word YET is my superpower.
Every expert was once a complete beginner.
I choose to be curious, not afraid.
Hard work beats talent when talent doesn't work hard.
I am building my brain right now.

for_parent.intro: Children who believe their intelligence can grow consistently outperform those who believe it is fixed — regardless of their current ability level. Here is how to build this mindset at home.

tips.title: Praise effort, not ability
tips.right: You worked really hard on that!
tips.why: Praising intelligence creates fear of failure — 'if I fail, maybe I'm NOT clever.' Praising effort teaches that success comes from trying.

tips.title: React to failure positively
tips.wrong: Don't worry, not everyone is good at maths
tips.right: This is tricky! What strategy could we try differently?
tips.why: Writing off an area as 'not for you' closes doors permanently. Reframing as 'not yet' keeps them open.

tips.title: Share your own struggles
tips.right: I find this hard too — let me try a different approach with you
tips.why: Children who see adults struggle and persist learn that struggle is normal, not a sign of inability.

tips.title: Celebrate process, not just results
tips.right: What did you learn today? What was the hardest thing? Did you ask for help when you needed it?
tips.why: Grades measure a moment. Process questions measure growth — which is what actually matters long-term.
